topic: Briefing on Treaty settlement legislation and Report of the Controller and Auditor-General, How public organisations are fulfilling Treaty settlemen
description: Wednesday 17 September 2025 | Māori Affairs Committee Briefing on Treaty settlement legislation and Report of the Controller and Auditor-General, How public organisations are fulfilling Treaty settlements Hearing of evidence Submitter list: Ministry of Housing and Urban Development and Kāinga Ora - Te Ariki Pihama, Deputy Chief Executive - Māori at Kainga Ora - Homes and Communities - Jessica Smith, Deputy Chief Executive Māori Crown Relations, Te Tūāpapa Kura Kāinga - Ministry of Housing and Urban Development - Glenn Phillips, Deputy Chief Executive, Organisational Performance, Te Tūāpapa Kura Kāinga - Ministry of Housing and Urban Development

welcome to all. Thank you very much, uh, for, for coming before us. Um, thank you for the opportunity for both the Ministry for Housing and Urban Development, and to collectively appear before you as the Mafi Select Committee and share how we are collectively working across the Housing and Urban Development System to meet our treaty settlement obligations. Uh, firstly I'd like to acknowledge the findings of the auditor general report and the message it sends that the Crown needs to do better in upholding the commitments made to II and HAPU through treaty settlements, uh, with direct responsibilities for the housing

and urban development outcomes. We recognize that our work sits at the heart of many of these commitments, and that our performance has a direct impact on the trust and wellbeing of Maori communities. Uh, treaty settlement commitments, um, are often referred to as commitments that are monitored through teta. Something I'm sure you are familiar with. Uh, it's important for the committee to note that when we, um, collectively talk about treaty settlement commitments, for us, it's more broad, it's more broader than that.

And so, yes, we include TETA commitments, but we also look at the pre and post settlement engagements, including those with ministerial accords relationship agreements, uh, and any other post settlement mechanisms that are in place. Um, as well as Maori community housing providers and any other iwi Maori organizations.

We are collectively working to ensure our treaty settlement commitments are not only tracked and but understood by, understood and honored in practice. Uh, this includes embedding ANA as a framework for accountability and transparency, building internal capability to understand and honor the intent behind each commitment, improving how we partner with Iwi and Hapu in the design and delivery of housing Urban Development Solutions, and working more closely with Kawa Uni, uh, to ensure we can bring synergies and efficiencies on how we manage

our treaty settlement commitments. Noting the dual role of uni corri as we work together across the housing and development system, the role of, uh, monitoring the public service.
